title: Algorapolis Civilization Stack
description: Schema for validating Algorapolis civilization layer definitions and their interdependencies. The Algorapolis civilization stack is a ten-layer architecture (6 core + 4 extended) that specifies how governance modalities, constitutional constraints, and civic domains interact within a modular governance framework. Designed by Goodluck Japhet Macha (independent researcher, Tanzania).

version.description: Semantic version of the civilization stack definition

layers.description: The ten civilization layers, ordered from foundational (1) to transcendent (10)

moduleMap.description: The Module Map defining which governance modality applies to which civic domain

constitutionalConstraints.description: The constitutional constraints: four guardrails and six human-reserved domains

operationalArchitecture.description: The four-layer operational architecture binding all subsystems

sleProperties.description: The five distinguishing properties of the Sovereign Logic Engine

GovernanceModality.title: GovernanceModality
GovernanceModality.description: The five governance modalities of Algorapolis. Each modality is matched to civic domains according to their nature, following the Matching Principle: governance modality must match domain nature.

LayerCategory.title: LayerCategory
LayerCategory.description: Whether a layer belongs to the core governance kernel or the extended civilization architecture

Guardrail.title: Guardrail
Guardrail.description: One of the four constitutional guardrails that bound all system capabilities

HumanReservedDomain.title: HumanReservedDomain
HumanReservedDomain.description: One of the six domains permanently reserved for human judgment, from which the SLE is excluded

CivicDomainName.title: CivicDomainName
CivicDomainName.description: The twelve civic domains of the Module Map

ZKPTier.title: ZKPTier
ZKPTier.description: The tiered zero-knowledge proof architecture for privacy-preserving governance

OperationalLayer.title: OperationalLayer
OperationalLayer.description: One of the four operational architecture layers

Layer.title: Layer
Layer.description: A single layer in the Algorapolis civilization stack

category.description: Whether this is a core (layers 1-6) or extended (layers 7-10) layer

description.description: What this layer provides and why it is necessary

subdomains.description: The specific topic areas within this layer

dependencies.description: Layer IDs that this layer depends upon (must be lower-numbered layers)

governanceModalities.description: Governance modalities active in this layer

constitutionalConstraints.description: Constitutional guardrails that are particularly operative in this layer

humanReservedDomains.description: Human-reserved domains that intersect with this layer (optional, only where applicable)

zkpTiers.description: ZKP tiers employed for privacy guarantees in this layer (optional)

operationalMapping.description: Which operational architecture layers this civilization layer maps to

CivicDomain.title: CivicDomain
CivicDomain.description: A civic domain in the Module Map with its assigned governance modality and rationale

primaryModality.description: The primary governance modality or hybrid combination, with rationale for the match

modalities.description: The governance modalities applied to this domain

rationale.description: Why this modality matches this domain's nature (the Matching Principle)

constitutionalConstraints.description: Constitutional guardrails that are operative in this domain

humanReservedDomains.description: Human-reserved domains that intersect with this civic domain

sleRole.description: What the Sovereign Logic Engine does within this domain (limited to execution, verification, and optimization — never decision-making in human-reserved areas)

ModuleMap.title: ModuleMap
ModuleMap.description: The Module Map: a specification of which governance modality applies to which civic domain, implementing Elinor Ostrom's polycentric governance through the Matching Principle

description.const: The Module Map specifies which governance modality applies to which civic domain, following the Matching Principle: governance modality must match domain nature.

civicDomains.description: The twelve civic domains and their governance modality assignments

ConstitutionalConstraints.title: ConstitutionalConstraints
ConstitutionalConstraints.description: The constitutional constraints of Algorapolis: four guardrails bounding system capabilities and six human-reserved domains permanently excluded from computational governance

enforcementMechanism.description: How this guardrail is computationally enforced

negativeSpecification.description: The real-world scenario this guardrail structurally prevents

enforcementMechanism.description: How this reservation is computationally enforced (Neutrality Axiom Extension, Specification Limit, Lean formal verification)

sleProhibition.description: What the SLE is specifically prohibited from doing in this domain

OperationalArchitecture.title: OperationalArchitecture
OperationalArchitecture.description: The four-layer operational architecture that binds all Algorapolis subsystems into a coherent whole

dependencies.description: Operational layers this layer depends upon (lower layers)

SLEProperties.title: SLEProperties
SLEProperties.description: The five distinguishing properties of the Sovereign Logic Engine that separate it from any form of artificial general intelligence

technicalBasis.description: The mathematical or engineering basis for this property

layers.name: Philosophy
layers.description: The intellectual foundations without which the technical architecture is mere engineering rather than a grounded response to civilizational necessity. Establishes why existing systems collapse and what principles must govern a successor architecture.

layers.name: Psychology
layers.description: Algorapolis is designed for real humans, not idealized rational agents. This layer grounds the architecture in empirical psychology: tribal identity, incentive structures, cognitive biases, and the Empathy Shield that makes mathematical governance an instrument of compassion.

layers.name: Governance
layers.description: How power flows in Algorapolis. The Module Map assigns governance modalities to civic domains. The Sovereign Logic Engine executes decisions without making them. Law as Code eliminates the gap between law-on-paper and law-in-practice. Liquid democracy enables continuous citizen sovereignty.

layers.name: Economics
layers.description: How resources flow in Algorapolis. Digital identity through W3C DIDs ensures self-sovereign identity. Privacy by mathematics (ZKPs, homomorphic encryption, differential privacy) provides guarantees stronger than any legal guarantee. Economic civilization design structures the Hybrid Economy and Universal Basic Prebate.

layers.name: Technology
layers.description: What infrastructure runs Algorapolis. The national-scale digital twin, IoT sensor networks, AI-powered demand forecasting, and automated allocation protocols. Mathematical foundations: Rust and Move for memory safety, BFT consensus, post-quantum cryptography, tiered ZKP architecture, and formal verification.

layers.name: Security
layers.description: How corruption and capture are prevented. Anti-corruption through Klitgaard's formula (C = M + D - A). Anti-dynasty systems with term limits and algorithmic monitoring. Constitutional locks as computationally enforced eternity clauses. Defense philosophy prioritizing resilience over conventional military dominance.

layers.name: Culture
layers.description: Why people believe in Algorapolis. Legitimacy is not a feature of governance architecture; it is the precondition for its operation. Civilizational memory, cultural legitimacy, artistic civilization, religious freedom through ZKPs, and multilingualism as epistemic necessity.

layers.name: Ecology
layers.description: Civilization in balance with nature. Planetary boundaries as constitutional constraints. Energy as a constitutional right. Nutritional adequacy as a computationally enforceable hard right. Steady-state economics and regenerative civilization.

layers.name: Civilization
layers.description: How Algorapolis governs civilization itself. Family and social fabric, time governance, science governance, disaster and extinction systems, emergence and unintended consequences, post-scarcity rights, and honest assessment of failure scenarios.

layers.name: Ark
layers.description: How civilization survives centuries and propagates through time. The Ark Protocol, adaptive civilizational scaling, the Human Continuity Archive, civilizational pluralism, propagation theory, and the transition architecture for deployment.

moduleMap.description: The Module Map specifies which governance modality applies to which civic domain, following the Matching Principle: governance modality must match domain nature.

civicDomains.name: healthcare
civicDomains.primaryModality: Hybrid: algorithmic resource allocation + democratic value setting
civicDomains.rationale: Resource optimization requires computation; care priorities require human values
civicDomains.sleRole: Optimizes resource allocation and scheduling; verifies constitutional compliance; does not determine care values or priorities

civicDomains.name: education
civicDomains.rationale: Curriculum is a value judgment; teacher competence is measurable
civicDomains.sleRole: Blind evaluation of educator competence; verifies qualification credentials via ZKPs; does not set curricula

civicDomains.name: infrastructure
civicDomains.rationale: Resource allocation is an optimization problem with constitutional constraints
civicDomains.sleRole: Full administrative execution: resource optimization, demand forecasting, automated allocation within constitutional bounds

civicDomains.name: civil_justice
civicDomains.rationale: Rights protection requires both computational enforcement and moral reasoning
civicDomains.sleRole: Constitutional compliance verification; due process enforcement; does not render moral judgments

civicDomains.name: criminal_justice
civicDomains.rationale: Due process requires both algorithmic fairness and community accountability
civicDomains.sleRole: Constitutional compliance checks; procedural fairness verification; mandatory human ratification before sentencing or benefit termination

civicDomains.name: economic_management
civicDomains.rationale: Allocation is computational; priorities are political
civicDomains.sleRole: Budget execution, procurement verification, benefit disbursement; does not set economic priorities

civicDomains.name: defense
civicDomains.rationale: Force deployment requires both constitutional compliance and democratic legitimacy
civicDomains.sleRole: Constitutional compliance verification; automated threat detection; does not authorize force deployment

civicDomains.name: environmental_management
civicDomains.rationale: Ecological constraints are non-negotiable and computationally enforceable
civicDomains.sleRole: Planetary boundary monitoring; constitutionally prohibited from authorizing activities that push boundaries beyond safe operating space

civicDomains.name: scientific_research
civicDomains.rationale: Peer review for quality; societal benefit for direction
civicDomains.sleRole: Blind evaluation of research proposals; automated replication infrastructure; does not determine research priorities

civicDomains.name: cultural_heritage
civicDomains.rationale: Culture must never be algorithmically optimized
civicDomains.sleRole: Constitutional protection enforcement; cultural preservation archival; does not evaluate, rank, or optimize cultural expression

civicDomains.name: international_relations
civicDomains.sleRole: Treaty compliance verification; diplomatic record management; does not set foreign policy direction

civicDomains.name: emergency_response
civicDomains.rationale: Speed requires computation; escalation requires human judgment
civicDomains.sleRole: Real-time resource coordination; automated early warning; mandatory human authorization for escalation decisions

guardrails.title: No Omniscience
guardrails.description: The system must never achieve or aspire to total knowledge of citizen behavior
guardrails.enforcementMechanism: Data minimization enforced by formal verification; ZKP architecture ensures the SLE processes proofs without accessing underlying data; temporal limitation with cryptographically enforced retention periods
guardrails.negativeSpecification: China's Social Credit System — centralized, opaque surveillance that makes citizens visible to the state while making the state invisible to citizens

guardrails.title: No Total Behavioral Prediction
guardrails.description: Predictive systems bounded to infrastructure, environment, and economic patterns — never individual behavior
guardrails.enforcementMechanism: Specification Limit Doctrine enforced in Lean Theorem Prover; predictive models limited to aggregate patterns with differential privacy (epsilon <= 0.5 for emotional data); constitutional rejection of individual-level behavioral prediction
guardrails.negativeSpecification: Predictive policing systems that perpetuate racial profiling through historical bias replication

guardrails.title: No Centralized Social Engineering
guardrails.description: The system must never manipulate citizen behavior toward state-defined outcomes
guardrails.enforcementMechanism: Human-reserved domains constitutionally excluded from SLE scope; Neutrality Axiom Extension prevents detection of religious/ideological identity in civic processes; formal verification of domain boundaries
guardrails.negativeSpecification: China's Social Credit System — punitive social scoring designed to shape citizen behavior toward state-defined norms

guardrails.title: No Unrestricted Computational Control
guardrails.description: The SLE remains bounded, auditable, and subject to human constitutional authority
guardrails.enforcementMechanism: Constitutional locks as computationally enforced eternity clauses; supermajority consensus across multiple independent verification nodes required for any modification; Constitutional Veto Layer requires human authorization for rights-affecting decisions
guardrails.negativeSpecification: The Dutch Childcare Benefits Scandal — automated authority without human oversight producing systematic injustice

humanReservedDomains.title: Religion and Spirituality
humanReservedDomains.description: The SLE must never define, judge, rank, or evaluate religious belief
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: Neutrality Axiom Extension: the governance infrastructure cannot detect religious or ideological identity in civic processes; ZKP-based blind evaluation protocols
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot access, process, classify, or make decisions based on religious affiliation, spiritual practice, or belief systems

humanReservedDomains.title: Culture and Identity
humanReservedDomains.description: Algorithmic optimization must never dictate cultural expression
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: Formal verification of domain boundaries in Lean Theorem Prover; cultural domains excluded from SLE optimization scope
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot evaluate, rank, optimize, or make allocation decisions based on cultural identity, ethnic affiliation, or cultural expression

humanReservedDomains.title: Family Structure
humanReservedDomains.description: The state must not define what constitutes a family
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: Specification Limit and constitutional locks; functional (not normative) definitions only for administrative purposes; human ratification required for family-related decisions
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot define, classify, rank, or make normative judgments about family structures, relationships, or domestic arrangements

humanReservedDomains.title: Moral Interpretation
humanReservedDomains.description: The SLE escalates moral questions to human judgment
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: Contested Formalization Protocol (three-team rule); Ambiguity Preservation Rule; mandatory human ratification for rights-affecting decisions
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot determine moral truth, resolve ethical dilemmas, or make decisions that require moral reasoning beyond procedural fairness

humanReservedDomains.title: Artistic Expression
humanReservedDomains.description: Art must never be optimized by governance systems
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: Constitutional exclusion of artistic domains from algorithmic evaluation; human-reserved status enforced by formal verification
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot evaluate, score, rank, allocate resources based on, or optimize artistic merit, aesthetic value, or creative expression

humanReservedDomains.title: Personal Identity
humanReservedDomains.description: Identity is a human right that computation can serve but never define
humanReservedDomains.enforcementMechanism: W3C DID self-sovereign identity; ZKP-based credential verification without identity disclosure; citizens own their data, control access, can revoke at any time
humanReservedDomains.sleProhibition: The SLE cannot define, classify, centralize, or make determinative decisions about personal identity; it can verify credentials via ZKPs but cannot access identity data

layers.name: Foundational Infrastructure
layers.description: The base layer providing spatial databases, IoT networks, offline resilience, and the technical substrate upon which all higher layers operate

layers.name: Reasoning & Simulation
layers.description: The analytical layer enabling economic telemetry, predictive governance, civilization simulation, and simulation-driven planning

layers.name: Testing & Validation
layers.description: The verification layer ensuring governance sandboxes validate policies before deployment and privacy architecture protects citizen data

layers.name: Operational Coordination
layers.description: The interface layer providing civic reporting, visualization, layered access, human-centered interfaces, and civilization-scale coordination

properties.name: Objective
properties.description: Based on set theory and Boolean laws, not training data. Decisions are derived from formal rules, not statistical patterns extracted from potentially biased datasets.
properties.technicalBasis: Set theory, Boolean logic, formally specified rules. No machine learning training data involved in decision derivation.

properties.name: Utility
properties.description: Like electricity or water infrastructure — provides a service without will. It cannot be lobbied, bribed, or socially influenced because it has no preferences to influence.
properties.technicalBasis: Deterministic execution engine with no objective function beyond executing published rules. No reward model, no preference learning.

properties.name: Narrow
properties.description: Manages resources; does not have will, consciousness, or preferences. It is not an AGI aspiring to sentience; it is a constrained computational tool for administrative problems.
properties.technicalBasis: Domain-specific rule engine with bounded computational scope. Cannot set its own goals; objectives are set by democratic deliberation (the Agora).

properties.name: Constrained
properties.description: Constitutional locks prevent scope expansion. The Engine cannot modify its own constitutional constraints, just as the laws of physics constrain physical systems.
properties.technicalBasis: Constitutional constraints encoded at a level inaccessible to the Engine itself. Formally verified in Lean 4 and Coq. Modification requires supermajority consensus across independent verification nodes.

properties.name: Auditable
properties.description: Every decision is logged, explainable through SHAP values, and reversible. Self-auditing protocols run continuous integrity checks verifying constitutional compliance.
properties.technicalBasis: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) for post-hoc interpretability. Complete audit trail for every consequential decision. Continuous self-auditing protocol with real-time publication of results.
